title: cenchrus orientalis subsp. triflorum
intro: Cenchrus orientalis subsp. triflorum, also known as sandbur or grassbur, is a type of invasive grass native to Asia and Africa. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The medicinal properties of this plant have been found to have health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and treating respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of Cenchrus orientalis subsp. triflorum include teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
